feedback.text: Dear Sir and Madam, i hope to see Digital Goods like Video Games added into the Digital Fairness Act. As there is already some news about the Stop Destroying Video Games initiative it would be great if those ideals would be added. In the recent years more and more goods mostly video games see a trend, where publishers or sellers reserve to right to deny access to the bought good by their choice at any given time. The consumer is in a defenseless position and only laws can prevent exploiting those loopholes. There are already cases, where physical goods like cars, e-bikes are forced to be connected to a server or won't be functional otherwise. The Seller reserves the right to shut down those servers at any given time, which would result in being all bought goods to be non-functional. Videos the prime example here. That is why i realy hope to see video games protected and usable for buyers at any given time by the digital fairness act. My second point i hope to see added in the act is consumer protection against unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns and addictive design of digital products in video games. Gambling mechanics like loot boxes or "loot box" like mechanic should be banned in video games. Also there should be no digital currency used in digital shops to obfuscate the money spend for goods. Instead there should only be real world currency used in the shop and labeled as such like Euro or Dollar. This would make prices more transparent and more easily comparable for consumer when they can A) Only directly buy a digital good and not with a chance to get the desired good and B) For a real world currency price tag like Euro or Dollar Sincerely, A concerned EU citizen.
